name: datamcp Agent Memory
description: Hosted MCP memory server for structured shared project context, with scoped remote links, curated canonical documents, append-only work logs, and compact handoffs.
tagline: Hosted structured project memory over MCP
approachDetail: Stores typed project-memory entries plus curated Rules and Project Summary documents. It does not provide semantic or vector retrieval.
searchDetail: PostgreSQL full-text search with text, date, tag, file, type, importance, and session filters. Archived entries are excluded by default.
storageDetail: Hosted by datamcp with separate project namespaces inside an Agent Memory connection.
mcpDetail: Exposes a hosted remote HTTPS MCP link with API-key authentication or OAuth 2.0 with PKCE.
toolSupportDetail: Works with clients and account tiers that support remote MCP transport and the required authentication flow.
scopesDetail: One connection can contain separate project namespaces, an MCP link can be scoped to one project, and entries can be grouped by registered session.
duplicateDetail: No semantic content deduplication. Clients can supply an idempotency key so retries of the same append do not create duplicate entries.
retentionDetail: Structured summaries can archive source entries from default search and handoff results without deleting them. There is no automatic TTL or importance decay.
multiUserDetail: Hosted projects can be shared through scoped links with Read Only, Read & Append, or Full Access permissions.
crossMachineDetail: The same hosted project memory is available to authenticated remote MCP clients on different machines.
webUiDetail: The datamcp dashboard manages Agent Memory connections, projects, canonical Rules and Project Summary documents, and scoped MCP links.
tokenBudgetingDetail: Search returns compact results, full entries are retrieved separately, and handoff tools build compact context from summaries plus recent or filtered active entries.
contextInjectionDetail: The connected client must call the memory tools. datamcp does not automatically capture every conversation or write after every task.
networkDetail: Agent Memory is a hosted service accessed through a remote HTTPS MCP endpoint.
auditTrailDetail: Work-log entries are append-only through the current MCP tools and include occurrence time, creator session, tags, files, type, importance, and metadata.
pros: Hosted remote MCP with no local memory server to operate
Shared project namespaces with scoped links and three permission levels
Curated Rules and Project Summary documents with a proposal and review path
Append-only structured work log with compact summaries and handoffs
Full-text and filtered retrieval without embedding infrastructure
cons: Requires network access and a datamcp account
No automatic conversation capture or task-completion writes
No edit or delete tools for work-log entries
